# Spoon MCP Server

Spoon MCP Server is a Java 21 Maven project that implements a stdio Model Context
Protocol (MCP) server for static architecture analysis of Java workspaces. It uses
[Spoon](https://spoon.gforge.inria.fr/) to parse and model Java sources, then exposes
architecture-oriented tools (component listing, dependency tracing, call graph extraction,
use case detection, data-flow tracing, runtime flow extraction, Mermaid rendering, graph
queries) and workflow prompts over JSON-RPC on stdin/stdout.

The server is designed for consumption by LLM agents and MCP-capable clients
(Claude Desktop, Claude Code, generic stdio MCP clients).

## At A Glance

- Language / runtime: Java 21
- Build tool: Maven 3.9+
- Entry point: `dev.dominikbreu.spoonmcp.Main`
- Packaging: shaded jar via `maven-shade-plugin` → `target/spoon-mcp-server.jar`
- Transport: stdio JSON-RPC 2.0 (MCP protocol version `2024-11-05`)
- Cache: `.spoon-mcp-cache/` in the working directory (JSON by default, optional graph backend)
- License: see `LICENSE`

## Documentation

- Project README: ./README.md
- Install & client wiring (Linux/macOS/Windows): ./docs/INSTALL.md
- MCP tool and prompt reference (arguments, examples): ./docs/TOOLS.md
- Architecture & package responsibilities: ./docs/ARCHITECTURE.md
- Graph-based source architecture POC: ./docs/SOURCE_ARCHITECTURE_POC.md
- Agent contributor guide: ./AGENTS.md
- Contributing guide: ./CONTRIBUTING.md
- Security policy: ./SECURITY.md
- Changelog: ./CHANGELOG.md
- JSON-RPC request examples: ./examples/jsonrpc/
  - `initialize.json`, `tools-list.json`, `index-workspace.json`, `render-flowchart.json`
  - `prompts-list.json`, `prompt-get-analyze-workspace.json`

## Source Map

- Maven project: ./pom.xml
- Main class: ./src/main/java/dev/dominikbreu/spoonmcp/Main.java
- MCP dispatch: ./src/main/java/dev/dominikbreu/spoonmcp/mcp/McpServer.java
- Tool implementations: ./src/main/java/dev/dominikbreu/spoonmcp/mcp/tools/
- Spoon-based scanners: ./src/main/java/dev/dominikbreu/spoonmcp/scanner/
- Component extractors: ./src/main/java/dev/dominikbreu/spoonmcp/extractor/
- Deployment / asset mergers: ./src/main/java/dev/dominikbreu/spoonmcp/merger/
- Architecture model: ./src/main/java/dev/dominikbreu/spoonmcp/model/
- Cache & graph projection: ./src/main/java/dev/dominikbreu/spoonmcp/cache/
  - `ModelCache.java`, `ArchitectureGraph.java`
- Mermaid renderers: ./src/main/java/dev/dominikbreu/spoonmcp/renderer/
  - flowchart, call flow, dependency map, dependency slice, source overview, use case timeline
- Tests: ./src/test/java/

## Capabilities

- Index one or more Java project roots with `index_workspace`. Indexing runs six passes:
  components + entrypoints, injection dependencies, call graph extraction (actual
  method invocations), data-flow tracing (parameter → sink paths), container inference,
  and external system inference.
- Detect REST endpoints, JMS consumers, Reactive Messaging consumers/producers,
  schedulers, EJB methods, services, repositories, entities, HTTP clients, and
  message-driven beans.
- Extract a **call graph** of directed method-call edges between components, with
  parameter-name mappings at each call site.
- Detect **use cases** automatically from indexed entrypoints: one use case per
  entrypoint, named by auto-derived heuristics or a JSON naming-config override,
  with a method call chain (or injection-based component list as fallback).
- Trace **data-flow paths** from entrypoint parameters to architectural sinks
  (persistence, messaging, http-outbound, event-bus), following parameter renames
  across call hops.
- Infer logical containers (API, service, repository, domain, messaging, scheduling).
- Merge deployment hints from Docker Compose and Ansible inventories.
- Trace component dependencies with configurable depth and condensation.
- Extract runtime flows for entry points using call-graph DFS when available, falling
  back to injection-edge BFS for pre-call-graph models.
- Render Mermaid flowcharts (system/container/module/component levels), call-flow
  diagrams with component-shape encoding and method-name edge labels, dependency maps,
  source overviews, focused dependency slices, and use-case Gantt timelines.
- Export architecture documentation (`export_architecture_docs`) and a graph-centric
  POC document (`export_graph_architecture_poc`).
- Query the architecture as a graph (`query_architecture_graph`) with
  `summary`, `find_nodes`, `find_edges`, `neighborhood`, `paths`, `impacted_by` actions.
- Expose workflow prompts through `prompts/list` and `prompts/get` for common
  multi-tool analysis tasks.

## MCP Tools

`index_workspace`, `list_apps`, `find_entrypoints`, `find_components`,
`get_component_dependencies`, `infer_containers`, `render_mermaid_flowchart`,
`get_runtime_flow`, `render_call_flow`, `render_use_case_timeline`, `render_source_overview`,
`render_dependency_map`, `render_component_dependency_diagram`,
`export_architecture_docs`, `export_graph_architecture_poc`,
`query_architecture_graph`, `explain_architecture`,
`detect_use_cases`, `trace_data_flow`, `render_pipeline`.

`render_pipeline` stitches multiple `DataFlowPath`s into a single end-to-end Mermaid
diagram across entrypoint boundaries (consumer → store → scheduler → channel → consumer).
Prefer it over `render_call_flow` (single entrypoint) and `trace_data_flow` (textual
per-path output) when the question is "what does the full pipeline look like".

See `./docs/TOOLS.md` for arguments and examples.

## MCP Prompts

Prompts are workflow templates for clients and agents; they do not replace tools.

- `analyze_workspace(path)`: index a workspace, list apps, inspect entrypoints/components,
  and summarize the architecture.
- `generate_architecture_docs(path, focusComponent)`: index a workspace and regenerate
  architecture docs plus graph POC docs.
- `investigate_component(component)`: inspect dependencies, graph neighborhood, and
  change impact for one component.
- `trace_use_case(entrypoint)`: follow runtime flow, call flow, data flow, and timeline
  views for an entrypoint/use case.
- `find_pipeline(filter)`: look for cross-entrypoint or messaging/store-linked pipeline
  chains.
- `architecture_view(app, view, maxNodes)`: render a projection-first component view from
  the indexed graph via `render_architecture_view` and optionally `export_likec4_model`.

## Build & Run

- Test: `mvn test`
- Package: `mvn package`
- Run: `java -jar target/spoon-mcp-server.jar`
- Smoke test: `java -jar target/spoon-mcp-server.jar < examples/jsonrpc/initialize.json`

## Configuration

- Cache backend (default `json`, optional `graph`):
  - Env: `SPOON_MCP_CACHE_BACKEND=graph`
  - JVM property: `-Dspoonmcp.cache.backend=graph`
- Cache directory: `.spoon-mcp-cache/` relative to the working directory.

## Release Tooling

- Maven Release Plugin configured for local Git releases (no remote push by default).
- JReleaser config at `./jreleaser.yml` targets `DerPate/spoon-mcp-server` on GitHub.
  - `JRELEASER_GITHUB_TOKEN` required for real publishing.

## Notes For Agents

- The cached architecture model is in-process; tools other than `index_workspace`
  require a previous `index_workspace` call (or a pre-existing `.spoon-mcp-cache/`).
- Stdio examples are JSONL-style request streams: one complete JSON-RPC object per
  physical line. Do not pipe pretty-printed multi-line JSON envelopes directly to stdin.
- Workflow prompts are intentionally coarse-grained. Add prompts for repeated
  multi-tool workflows, not one prompt per tool.
- `detect_use_cases` and `trace_data_flow` produce richer output when call-graph data
  is available (i.e. after `index_workspace` on a real project). Without call edges they
  fall back to injection-dependency traversal and emit a warning label.
- Messaging entrypoints/interfaces resolve `broker` from
  `mp.messaging.{incoming|outgoing}.{channel}.connector`. Channels with no `connector`
  property that are referenced by both an `@Incoming` and an `@Outgoing` declaration in
  the same module are tagged `IN_MEMORY` (no external broker, no `ExternalSystem`).
  The resolved broker-side destination (`mp.messaging.*.topic` / `.address` /
  `.queue.name` / `.exchange.name`) is exposed as `topic` on the entry.
- `trace_data_flow`: every `store` sink carries `linkedPathIds` listing downstream
  `DataFlowPath`s whose entrypoint reads the same shared field — explicit
  cross-entrypoint pipeline links so agents do not need to match field names heuristically.
- Shared-state reads through accessor calls are evidence too: if a scheduler/service
  calls another component method that returns a shared field directly or invokes a
  method on it (for example `return cache` or `return cache.keySet()`), extraction
  records a caller-side `field-access-via-getter` read owned by the callee component.
- `trace_data_flow`: edges now also carry `assignedToVar` + `returnsTracked` for
  invocations whose result is bound to a local (used to extend tracking when the callee
  returns a parameter), and `killedTrackedNames` listing caller locals reassigned before
  the call site (tracker drops tracking when the name appears).
- `trace_data_flow`: new sink kinds `file-outbound` and `object-storage`. Recognised
  either via component stereotype or via {@code ArchitectureModel.outboundSinkSites},
  which records direct invocations against `java.nio.file.Files`,
  `software.amazon.awssdk.services.s3.*`, and `com.azure.storage.*` even when the
  callee is not a project component. New entrypoint types `EVENT_BUS_CONSUMER` (Vert.x
  `eventBus.consumer(addr, handler)`), `WEBSOCKET_ENDPOINT`, `SSE_ENDPOINT`, `GRPC_METHOD`
  — Vert.x EventBus, WebSocket, SSE (REST + `text/event-stream` / `SseEventSink`), and
  gRPC (`@GrpcService` / `BindableService` / `*Grpc.*ImplBase` stubs) all detected.
- `trace_data_flow`: call-edge `paramMapping` now also descends into constructor calls,
  nested invocations, and ternaries (`new Foo(x)`, `wrap(x)`, `cond ? x : y`); callee
  parameter names whose mapping was synthesised this way are listed in the new
  `syntheticParamMappings` set on the edge.
- `trace_data_flow`: `MESSAGING_PRODUCER` and `SCHEDULER` entrypoints also seed tracking
  from transitively read shared-state fields even when they declare parameters of their
  own, and writes whose RHS is a field read on the same bean record `sourceFieldName` so
  store sinks stay attached to paths tracking the upstream field. Field types `Logger`,
  `Log`, `Slf4j`, `Tracer`, and types prefixed with `Audit` are excluded from
  shared-state heuristics.
- Property graph (`query_architecture_graph`): projects `DataFlowPath`,
  `DataFlowSink`, and `PipelineChain` vertices and `ORIGINATES`, `REACHES`,
  `ON_FIELD`, `AT_COMPONENT`, `LINKS_TO`, `WORKFLOW_LINK`, and `HAS_SEGMENT` edges.
  `LINKS_TO` materialises `linkedPathIds` for `store`, `messaging`, and `event-bus`
  sinks (carries `linkKind` plus `viaField` + `fieldOwnerComponentId` for STORE,
  `viaChannel` for MESSAGING / EVENT_BUS). `PipelineChain` nodes carry
  `segmentCount`, `rootEntrypointId`, and `linkKinds` (comma-separated handoff
  kinds in traversal order); each `HAS_SEGMENT` edge carries `segmentIndex` and,
  for non-root segments, `linkKind` + `incomingSinkId` + the channel/field
  property — giving graph-only clients parity with `render_pipeline`.
- Prefer `WORKFLOW_LINK` over reconstructing pipelines from raw `linkedPathIds`.
  `WORKFLOW_LINK` is the canonical query edge for messaging/event-bus/state-handoff
  continuation between `DataFlowPath` nodes.
- Pipeline rendering is evidence-driven. Prefer `WORKFLOW_LINK` edges over reconstructing
  chains manually. For Spring projects, `MESSAGING` links come from producer sink metadata
  (`KafkaTemplate.send`, listener topic resolution) and `PERSISTENCE_HANDOFF` links come
  from repository write/read entity matching. If `render_pipeline` returns diagnostics
  instead of Mermaid, inspect unresolved messaging destinations and persistence write/read
  counts first.
- Plain Java object flow is source-derived through `ObjectFlowIndex`. Prefer call edges
  with `receiverEvidence` and `receiverConfidence` over guessing from class names.
  Polymorphic receiver expansion is capped at 25 implementation targets by default; if
  `receiverExpansionCapped=true`, report that the flow is conservative.
- Workflow root/link semantics live in `workflow/`, not in renderers. When pipeline,
  runtime flow, use-case, or architecture-view output looks wrong, inspect
  `WorkflowTraversalPolicy`, `WorkflowLinker`, and `WorkflowGraphBuilder` first.
- Property graph component scoring is noise-aware. Prefer components with
  `workflowRelevant=true`, `businessRelevant=true`, high `workflowBridgeScore`, and
  low `noiseScore`. Treat high fan-in utilities, formatters, parsers, mappers,
  loggers, config classes, DTO-ish types, and `UNKNOWN` components as supporting
  infrastructure unless they also bridge entrypoints, sinks, state, messaging, or
  external systems.
- Gradle and Maven project shape is owned by `dev.dominikbreu.spoonmcp.build`.
  Do not reintroduce build traversal in `SpoonScanner` or framework extractors.
- Spring Boot extraction lives in `SpringExtractor` and uses `SpringConfigResolver`
  for bounded `application.properties` / YAML evidence.
- Gradle support is first-class at the metadata boundary. Literal parsing is the
  current non-executing strategy; richer Gradle source sets, artifact tasks,
  dependency metadata, convention plugins, and included builds belong in the
  Gradle detector rather than scattered call sites.
- Prefer projection tools before hand-drawing architecture diagrams:
  `render_architecture_view` for Mermaid/C4-style component diagrams and
  `export_likec4_model` when LikeC4-compatible context is useful. Treat these
  outputs as graph projections. Do not add invented actors, components, or edges
  unless clearly marked as interpretation outside the MCP graph.
- Property graph state handoffs are first-class component edges:
  `WRITES_STATE`, `READS_STATE`, and `STATE_HANDOFF`. Use `STATE_HANDOFF` and
  `PipelineChain`/`WORKFLOW_LINK` before inferring workflow pivots from fan-in alone.
  Self-only state reads/writes are local implementation detail and should not outrank
  cross-component handoffs or workflow/business components.
- Tool argument schemas in `McpServer.java` are the authoritative source — keep
  `docs/TOOLS.md` in sync when changing them.
- Generated artifacts (`target/`, `.spoon-mcp-cache/`, `dependency-reduced-pom.xml`)
  are git-ignored and must not be committed.
- This repo intentionally ships no GitHub Actions or pre-commit hooks.
- Code style is imperative Java 21 (no Mutiny / reactive). Use constructor injection
  in beans and JBoss/Quarkus logging only when adding logging.
